title: The Green Yard
text:
Join us as we explore food forests, edible landscapes, and the inspiring
      people creating abundance through plants and food in different places.
    

    
      
        Explore the Tours
      

      
        ▶ Watch on YouTube
      
    

  






  


    
    


      
      

        Featured Tour

        
          Touring a Four-Year-Old Tropical Food Forest in Phoenix
        

        
          Step inside a four-year-old Phoenix food forest and discover how
          tropical fruit trees, thoughtful design, and dense planting create
          abundance in the desert.
        

        
          
            ▶ Watch Now
          
        

        

          

          ▶

        

      


      
      

        Most Recent Tour

        
          A Florida Food Forest Packed with Tropical Diversity
        

        
          Explore KC’s densely planted Florida food forest, where tropical fruit
          trees, edible plants, and unusual species grow together on just
          0.17 acres.
        

        
          
            ▶ Watch Now
          
        

        

          

          ▶

        

      

    


    
    

      Explore by Location

      Discover Abundance Around the World

      
        Explore food forests, edible landscapes, farms, and community gardens
        through the people and places that make each location unique.

title: Tropical Trees
text:
Tropical Trees
Welcome to our guide on plants that thrive in the Phoenix area! Here, you’ll discover a variety of plants perfectly suited to the unique desert climate. Click on each image to explore more about how to grow, care for, and enjoy these resilient plants in your garden.









Mexican Papaya
Fruit Punch Mango

title: About The Green Yard
text:
Planting a Sweet Granadilla




My name is Schuyler Hamilton and I am a native Arizonan. I absolutely love Phoenix and after living in mutiple states around the country during College I decided to come back to my home. Growing up my mom used to garden, we would grow all kinds of vegetables and they also had several mature citrus trees (Navel Orange, Grapefruit and Mandarin) in their Green Yard. This exposed me to the world of growing your own food. During High School and College I got away from gardening and focusing on other aspects of life including Photography and Videography. My oldest son was born in 2018 and my life was never the same again. Not only did he change every aspect of living but I also awakened my passion for growing my own food. In early spring of 2019 I bought my first Banana plants (Blue Java and Dwarf Nam Wah) and planted them in our old Green Yard.

Implemented new strategies that increased efficiency by 30%.
Led a team of 10 professionals in achieving project milestones.




Office Team







Studied Computer Science with a focus on artificial intelligence and machine learning.



University Campus






In my free time, I enjoy:
